## Question and Solutions

### 1- What is the total number of customers?

```
SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_customers
FROM customers;
```
<img width="181" height="85" alt="Screenshot 2026-07-16 163008" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7e9d37b5-9b7e-4df0-a40b-37e6d75d62aa" />


### 2- How many orders have been placed on the platform?

```
SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_orders
FROM orders;
```
<img width="142" height="58" alt="Screenshot 2026-07-16 163140" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/70746929-a4e3-4cca-8b90-2cf533c281a7" />


### 3- What is the total revenue generated by the business?

```
SELECT ROUND(SUM(total_price),2) AS total_revenue
FROM orders;
```
<img width="151" height="65" alt="Screenshot 2026-07-16 163306" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/56e96612-bbdd-49c9-a0cc-5b54815ff61e" />

### 4- Which customers purchased products from the highest number of different categories?

```
SELECT
c.customer_id,
CONCAT(c.first_name, ' ', c.last_name) AS customer_name,
COUNT(DISTINCT p.category) AS categories_purchased
FROM customers c
JOIN orders o
ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id
JOIN order_items oi
ON o.order_id = oi.order_id
JOIN products p
ON oi.product_id = p.product_id
GROUP BY c.customer_id, customer_name
ORDER BY categories_purchased DESC
LIMIT 10;
```

<img width="442" height="252" alt="Screenshot 2026-07-17 124357" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d7ded565-8c1d-4ff4-894a-dc9cc76d24ae" />


### 5- How many orders has each customer placed, and how much have they spent in total?

```
select c.customer_id,concat(c.first_name,' ',c.last_name) as customer_name,
count(o.order_id) as total_orders,
round(sum(o.total_price),2) as total_spent
from customers c
join orders o
on c.customer_id=o.customer_id
group by c.customer_id,customer_name
order by total_spent desc
limit 10;
```

<img width="512" height="282" alt="Screenshot 2026-07-16 164006" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9fecdc37-b291-4ffa-ab4e-9bfc3b8dd30f" />

Only the first few rows are shown below. The complete query returns all customers.

### 6- Which product categories generate the highest revenue?
```
select p.category,round(sum(oi.price_at_purchase),2) as total_revenue
from products p
join order_items oi
on p.product_id=oi.product_id
group by p.category
order by total_revenue desc;
```
<img width="263" height="131" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a74e1952-df5b-430d-8396-07e43aae29c5" />

### 7- Which suppliers contribute the highest sales revenue?
```
select s.supplier_name,round(sum(oi.quantity*oi.price_at_purchase),2) as total_sales
from suppliers s
join products p
on s.supplier_id=p.supplier_id
join order_items oi
on  p.product_id=oi.product_id
group by s.supplier_name
order by total_sales desc
limit 5;
```
<img width="347" height="156" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/fe34a584-e543-4a89-a1db-de97547eeb85" />

### 8- What is the average delivery time(in days) for each shipping carrier?

```
select carrier,
round(avg(datediff(delivery_date,shipment_date)),2) as avg_delivery_time
from shipments
where delivery_date is not null
group by carrier
order by avg_delivery_time;

SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_orders
FROM orders;
```
<img width="232" height="108" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/61743cc9-6b1f-469c-aa2e-a4a49937df51" />

### 9- Which customers spend more than the average customer?

```
SELECT
    c.customer_id,
    CONCAT(c.first_name,' ',c.last_name) AS customer_name,
    ROUND(SUM(o.total_price),2) AS total_spent
FROM customers c
JOIN orders o
ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id
GROUP BY c.customer_id, customer_name
HAVING SUM(o.total_price) >
(
    SELECT AVG(customer_total)
    FROM
    (
        SELECT SUM(total_price) AS customer_total
        FROM orders
        GROUP BY customer_id
    ) AS avg_spending
)
ORDER BY total_spent DESC;
```
<img width="353" height="407" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/237f9d12-366b-4cc8-a8fd-0442e3e439b8" />

Only the first few rows are shown below. The complete query returns all customers.

### 10- Which products are priced above the average product price?
```
select product_name,price
from products
where price>(
select  avg(price) from products) order by price desc;
```

<img width="242" height="282" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0da74ac0-cda1-49ab-9698-859a35de1330" />

Only the first few rows are shown below.

### 11- How has revenue changed month by month?

```
with monthly_revenue as
(select date_format(order_date,'%Y-%m') as Month,
round(sum(total_price),2) as revenue
from orders
group by Month)
select * 
from monthly_revenue
order by revenue desc;
```

<img width="210" height="322" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a9baa2d6-fe69-43c7-ba3e-059d6f4a9a0b" />

### 12-Which customers generate the highest revenue for the business?

```
WITH customer_spending AS (
    SELECT
        c.customer_id,
        CONCAT(c.first_name, ' ', c.last_name) AS customer_name,
        ROUND(SUM(o.total_price), 2) AS total_spent
    FROM customers c
    JOIN orders o
        ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id
    GROUP BY c.customer_id, customer_name
)

SELECT
    customer_id,  customer_name,total_spent,
    DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY total_spent DESC) AS customer_rank
FROM customer_spending
ORDER BY customer_rank;
```

<img width="456" height="421" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/38541449-fe30-49de-910f-2b02945de2eb" />

### 13- Identify the Top 10 products based on total sales revenue.

```
SELECT
    p.product_name,
    ROUND(SUM(oi.quantity * oi.price_at_purchase),2) AS total_sales
FROM products p
JOIN order_items oi
ON p.product_id = oi.product_id
GROUP BY p.product_id, p.product_name
ORDER BY total_sales DESC
LIMIT 10;
```
<img width="270" height="255" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3194a739-b024-4e1c-8078-c7f90d3f9522" />

### 14- Identify products with an average review rating below 3.5 but revenue above the overall average product revenue.

```

WITH product_revenue AS (
    SELECT
p.product_id,p.product_name,
ROUND(SUM(oi.quantity * oi.price_at_purchase), 2) AS revenue
FROM products p
JOIN order_items oi
ON p.product_id = oi.product_id
GROUP BY p.product_id, p.product_name
),

product_rating AS (
SELECT
product_id,ROUND(AVG(rating), 2) AS avg_rating
FROM reviews
GROUP BY product_id
)
SELECT
pr.product_id,pr.product_name,pr.revenue,rt.avg_rating
FROM product_revenue pr
JOIN product_rating rt
ON pr.product_id = rt.product_id
WHERE rt.avg_rating < 3.5
AND pr.revenue >
(SELECT AVG(revenue)
FROM product_revenue)
ORDER BY pr.revenue DESC;

```
<img width="468" height="407" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b4f536ba-d73b-4ff5-87f6-37f3eeb68e82" />
